svg.title: RNTuple internal memory layout
svg.desc: Structural diagram showing the four layers of an RNTuple file: file envelope (header + footer), cluster groups, clusters, and pages (the actual data). Fields map to columns which map to pages.

g.text: key–value store, one RNTuple anchor key

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is stored in the RNTuple header envelope?')
sendPrompt('What is stored in the RNTuple footer envelope?')
sendPrompt('How are RNTuple fields different from TTree branches?')
sendPrompt('What are RNTuple columns and how do they differ from fields?')

g.text: Spans a range of entries; locators in footer

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is a column in RNTuple and how is it stored?')
sendPrompt('What is a page in RNTuple? How large is a page?')
sendPrompt('How are pages compressed in RNTuple?')
sendPrompt('Can different columns use different compression in RNTuple?')
sendPrompt('What is RNTupleCardinality and what column type stores nMuon?')
sendPrompt('What is a cluster summary in RNTuple?')
sendPrompt('Are pages in different clusters independent? Can you read one cluster without reading others?')
sendPrompt('How does uproot find which cluster to read for a given entry range?')
